perturbation.original_text: 2. Definition. "Confidential Information" means any information, technical data, or know - how, inc luding, but not limited to, that which relates to research, product plans, products, services, customers, markets, opt -in information including Short -code and URL information, marketing or finances, which Confidential Information is designated in writing t o be confidential or proprietary, or if given orally, is confirmed promptly in writing as having been disclosed as confidential or proprietary.
perturbation.changed_text: 2. Definition. "Confidential Information" means information. All information is subject to this agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The original definition of 'Confidential Information' provided specific criteria and examples. The modified definition removes these criteria, broadly defining it as 'information' and stating that 'All information is subject to this agreement'. This introduces an in-text contradiction because later clauses rely on a more restricted definition of confidential information, creating uncertainty about which information is truly protected. It contradicts the implicit understanding that only certain types of information, as originally defined, are subject to the NDA's restrictions.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3. Non -Disclosure of Confidential Information. The Recipient agrees not to use the Confidential Information for any purpose other than that s et forth in Section 1 of this Agreement. The Recipient will not disclose any Confidential Information to third parties except those directors, officers, employees, consultants and agents of Recipient who are required to have the information in order to car ry out the purpose set forth in Section 1 of this Agreement. Recipient has had or will have directors, officers, employees, consultants and agents of Recipient to whom Confidential Information is disclosed or who have access to Confidential Information sig n a Non -Disclosure Agreement in content substantially similar to this Agreement and will promptly notify the Discloser in writing of the names of each such person who has signed such agreements after such agreements are signed. Recipient agrees that it wil l take all reasonable measures to protect the secrecy of and avoid disclosure or use of Confidential Information in order to prevent it from falling into the public domain or the possession of persons other than those persons authorized hereunder to have a ny such information, which measures shall include the highest degree of care that Recipient utilizes to protect its own Confidential Information of a similar nature. Recipient agrees to notify the Discloser in writing of any misuse or misappropriation of s uch Confidential Information which may come to its attention.
perturbation.justification: The original text defined 'Confidential Information' with specific criteria. The changed text broadens the definition to 'information' without these criteria. This contradicts Section 3, which relies on the original definition to restrict the use and disclosure of 'Confidential Information.' By making all information subject to the agreement, the changed text creates an ambiguity as to what information is actually protected, contradicting the intended scope and protection outlined in Section 3.

perturbation.original_text: 3. Non -Disclosure of Confidential Information. The Recipient agrees not to use the Confidential Information for any purpose other than that s et forth in Section 1 of this Agreement. The Recipient will not disclose any Confidential Information to third parties except those directors, officers, employees, consultants and agents of Recipient who are required to have the information in order to car ry out the purpose set forth in Section 1 of this Agreement. Recipient has had or will have directors, officers, employees, consultants and agents of Recipient to whom Confidential Information is disclosed or who have access to Confidential Information sig n a Non -Disclosure Agreement in content substantially similar to this Agreement and will promptly notify the Discloser in writing of the names of each such person who has signed such agreements after such agreements are signed. Recipient agrees that it wil l take all reasonable measures to protect the secrecy of and avoid disclosure or use of Confidential Information in order to prevent it from falling into the public domain or the possession of persons other than those persons authorized hereunder to have a ny such information, which measures shall include the highest degree of care that Recipient utilizes to protect its own Confidential Information of a similar nature. Recipient agrees to notify the Discloser in writing of any misuse or misappropriation of s uch Confidential Information which may come to its attention.
perturbation.changed_text: 3. Non -Disclosure of Confidential Information. The Recipient agrees not to use the Confidential Information for any purpose other than that s et forth in Section 1 of this Agreement. The Recipient will not disclose any Confidential Information to any parties.
perturbation.explanation: The original section allowed for disclosure of 'Confidential Information' to specific individuals (directors, officers, employees, consultants, agents) under certain conditions (they need the information to carry out the purpose of Section 1 and have signed a similar NDA). The change removes this exception, stating that the Recipient will not disclose 'Confidential Information' to 'any parties'. This creates an in-text contradiction if Recipient's agents need access to information as stated in Section 1.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1. Purpose. The Discloser has agreed to make available to the Recipient certain Confidential Information (as defined below) of the Discloser for the purpose of evaluating a possible business transaction with the Discloser.
perturbation.justification: The original text allows for disclosure of confidential information to specific individuals (directors, officers, employees, consultants, agents) if they require the information to fulfill the purpose outlined in Section 1 and have signed a similar NDA. The changed text removes this exception, stating that the Recipient will not disclose 'Confidential Information' to 'any parties'. This creates a direct contradiction with Section 1, which implies that the Recipient's agents might need access to confidential information to evaluate a possible business transaction.

perturbation.original_text: 5. Return of Materials. Any materials or documents of which have been furnished by the Discloser to the Recipient will be promptly returned, accompanied by copies of such doc umentation, after the evaluation set forth in Section 1 of this Agreement has been concluded.
perturbation.changed_text: 5. Return of Materials. Any materials or documents of which have been furnished by the Discloser to the Recipient will be promptly returned after the evaluation set forth in Section 1 of this Agreement has been concluded.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause required returning materials 'accompanied by copies of such documentation'. The modified version removes the phrase 'accompanied by copies of such documentation'. This implies that only the originals are to be returned. This is an in-text contradiction if, for example, the Recipient needs to retain copies for their internal records related to the 'possible business transaction' mentioned in Section 1, due to legal compliance purposes.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1. Purpose. The Discloser has agreed to make available to the Recipient certain Confidential Information (as defined below) of the Discloser for the purpose of evaluating a possible business transaction with the Discloser.
perturbation.justification: The original clause requires returning materials 'accompanied by copies of such documentation', which implies that the recipient can retain copies. The changed version removes this phrase, implying that only the original documents are to be returned and no copies are allowed. This contradicts Section 1, which states the purpose is to evaluate a 'possible business transaction'. The explanation suggests that the Recipient might need to retain copies for their internal records related to the 'possible business transaction' due to legal compliance purposes. Therefore, removing the provision for keeping copies creates a direct conflict and uncertainty, making enforcement difficult.

perturbation.original_text: 6. No License Granted. Nothing in this Agreement is intended to grant any rights to Recipient under any patent, copyright, trade secret or other intellectual prop erty right nor shall this Agreement grant Recipient any rights in or to the other party's Confidential Information, except the limited right to review such Confidential Information solely for the purpose set forth in Section 1 of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 6. No License Granted. Nothing in this Agreement is intended to grant any rights to Recipient under any patent, copyright, trade secret or other intellectual prop erty right nor shall this Agreement grant Recipient any rights in or to the other party's Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: The original section explicitly grants a 'limited right to review' Confidential Information for the purpose stated in Section 1. The modification removes this exception. This is contradictory to Section 1, which explicitly states the purpose is to evaluate a possible business transaction that inherently requires reviewing the confidential information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1. Purpose. The Discloser has agreed to make available to the Recipient certain Confidential Information (as defined below) of the Discloser for the purpose of evaluating a possible business transaction with the Discloser.
perturbation.justification: The original text in Section 6 granted a limited right to review Confidential Information for the purpose stated in Section 1. The changed text in Section 6 removes this explicit allowance. Section 1 explicitly states the purpose is to evaluate a business transaction, which inherently requires reviewing confidential information. By removing the exception in Section 6, it contradicts the purpose outlined in Section 1, thus creating a strong in-text contradiction as stated in the Perturbation Explanation.
